Changed By Date Version Notes: Mitchell Bluford 12/01/2021 0.0 Original document EX30 – Cigarette Fast- and Slow-Movers Updates McLane manages the allocation logic and allocation types for cigarettes within WMOS. Cigarettes in pre-stamped jurisdiction s require the ability to move between slow-moving allocation logic and fast-moving allocation logic. For this reason, a custom flag will be added at the Pick Location Item level to indicate that cigarette in a pre-stamped jurisdiction is either a fast-mover or a slow-mover . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Added drop-down on Pick Location Items UI MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N McLane cigarette divisions require the ability to transition cigarettes between fast- and slow-movers in WMO S . Slow - moving cigarettes allocate at a less than case level, to never stamp excess inventory, and fast-moving cigarettes replenish only at a case level, allowing excess stamped inventory to be held in pre-stamped active cigarette locations (allocation functionality is found in MC L N EX01 & EX11 extension specs). Slow- and fast-moving cigarettes are held in pre-stamped jurisdiction s , where cigarette s are stamped during the replenishment process. A cigarette ’s status as a fast- or slow-mover is a relationship that exists between a specific cigarette SKU and a specific jurisdiction. Meaning , a cigarette SKU being sold to a KY jurisdiction, may be a fast-mover, while the same cigarette SKU being sold to an OH jurisdiction may be a slow-mover. Pre-stamped c igarette jurisdictions are separated in WMOS by work areas and a specific cigarette SKU is only slotted one time per jurisdiction. For these reason s , WMOS is customized to add a flag at the Pick Location Items level to denote whether a cigarette SKU in relation to its jurisdiction is a fast-mover or a slow-mover. The McLane host system sends order lines to WMOS with allocation type s denoting specific jurisdictions for the ordered SKU . Managing this fast- and slow-moving transition process inside of WMOS requires that host system be alerted to this change . At the time this change is made in WMOS, a custom PIX message is sent to the host system . When the host system receives the PIX, the allocation type associated with this SKU and jurisdiction relationship is changed for all order lines sent to WMOS from that point forward. Assumptions A cigarette can be a fast-over in one jurisdiction and a slow-mover in another . A cigarette can only be either a fast-mover or a slow-mover in a single jurisdiction at one time. Fast- and slow-moving functionality only applies to pre-stamped jurisdictions . Any order lines that are already in WMOS will not be updated from this flag . This flag will not be changeable while there are active order lines of the same SKU/allocation type in the system ; the updates will not occur. This flag will not be changeable while there are open picking or replenishment tasks for this particular SKU and pre-stamped jurisdiction (INT3, INT99, INT50). Slow-moving allocation types and fast-moving allocation types share the same three letter code . The fast-moving allocation type is entered as all capital letters, e.g., ‘AFQ’, and the slow-moving allocation type is stored as ‘afq’. An allocation type must have both the capital fast-mover allocation type and the lowercase slow-mover allocation type to be eligible for these updates. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX11 & EX01 Cigarette Allocation Customizations – works in conjunction with EX11 & EX01 by driving the cigarette allocation logic of whether a cigarette allocates via fast-moving logic or slow-moving logic. Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Process Steps : User navigates to Pick Location UI and filters for specific location /item needing allocation type update. User selects location, select s Pick Location Items . User selects the applicable Pick Location Item record for the cigarette SKU and correct jurisdiction. User navigates to Item Attributes section and selects option from “Cigarette Fast/Slow Mover” drop-down to select either “FAST” or “SLOW”. User selects save. If all custom validations are met, WMOS updates the applicable tables and sends the custom PIX to the host. UI Pick Location Items UI – Item Attributes section, Fast- or Slow- M oving U pdate To execute the update from slow-moving to fast-moving or vice versa, the user will enter the Pick Location Item record for the SKU /P ick L ocation in question and adjust a drop-down option to designate the cigarette as FAST or SLOW moving and then Save the change. At the point of Saving the change, if all validations are passed, WMOS will send a custom PIX to the host system alerting it of the change of allocation style for the cigarette SKU and jurisdiction. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global extension flag must be enabled. This is controlled by the custom system code C-000-000 parameter byte 1. This is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’ , WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, the global extension flag is turned off and therefore only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C-000- EX30 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, this extension is disabled, and no custom processing will occur. Third, EX01 and EX11 cigarette allocation extensions must also be enabled. These are controlled through the custom system codes C-000-EX01 and C-000-EX11. Both are configured as Y/N flags and if both are configured as ‘Y’ then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, this extension is disabled, and no custom processing will occur. IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement IF C-000- EX30 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement) IF C-000-EX01 byte 1 = Y (EX01 cigarette allocation enablement) IF C-000-EX11 byte 1 = Y (EX11 cigarette allocation enablement) ELSE update functionality detailed in this document is disabled ELSE update functionality detailed in this document is disabled ELSE update functionality detailed in this document is disabled ELSE update functionality detailed in this document is disabled Va lidations When the user attempts to save the update, WMOS first checks the associated SKU’s ITEM_CBO.PROD_TYPE against the custom system code C-EX1-01 byte 1-3. If the cigarette SKU’s Product Type is not = C-EX1-01 byte 1-3, then WMOS will throw custom error CUST/ 300 1 , “Error: Associated SKU is not a cigarette. Incorrect Product Type” . Otherwise, WMOS proceeds to the next validation. Next, WMOS checks the allocation type in Allocation Type system code B-325 with the same configured value as the PICK_LOCN_DTL.SKU_ATTR_1 field as the pick location detail which is being edited. If the allocation type in B-325 has byte 2 enabled (checkbox), then WMOS will proceed to the next validation. If the associated allocation type byte 2 is not enabled, then WMOS will throw custom error CUST/ 300 2 , “Error: Allocation Type not enabled for EX30 Fast/Slow Moving Updates” and will not proceed with custom updates. After , WMOS checks if there are any open task details in the system for replenishment to the pick location, or picking from the pick location (INT3, INT99, INT50). If there are any task details in less than < 90 status (less than completed or canceled) with replenishment of INT3 or INT99 with a destination location of the pick location OR any task details in < 90 status of INT50 with a pull location of the pick location, then WMOS will throw custom error message CUST/ 300 3 , “Error: O pen task details for pick location”. Otherwise, WMOS will proceed to the next validation. As an additional check against tasks or open allocations, WMOS checks whether or not a single Pick Location D etail exists for the Pick Location in question. Multiple P ick L ocation details in a pre-stamped jurisdiction indicate incomplete tasks for the Pick Location or unresolved flows in EX01/11. If there are multiple pick location details for the same location ID, then WMOS will throw custom error message CUST/ 300 4 , “Error: M ultiple pick location item records exist – check open tasks/allocations, if none exist, delete the blank I tem A ttribute 1 P ick L ocation I tem record”. Otherwise , WMOS proceeds to the next validation. To be sure that the updated Allocation Type has allocation logic set up, WMOS will also check that Allocation Priorities exist for the updated Allocation Type . If Allocation Priorities do not exist for the updated Allocation Type for INT 3, INT9, INT50, and INT99 , then WMOS will throw custom error message, CUST/ 300 5 , “Error: Allocation Priorities not fully configured for updated Allocation Type”. Otherwise WMOS proceeds to the next validation. Last , WMOS checks order lines that have already been bridged into WMOS . If any order lines exist for the combination of the SKU and allocation type in question and they are in < Packed status (150), then WMOS will throw custom error message CUST/ 300 6 , “Error: U nprocessed order lines for current allocation type” . Otherwise , WMOS proceeds with custom updates . Updates The following logic is used to update a cigarette SKU / jurisdiction relationship from a slow-mover to a fast-mover. This update will occur after a user edits a Pick Location Item record , selecting the drop-down option of FAST and then selects SAVE and all validations have passed. Pick Location Items Update update pick_locn_dtl set sku_attr_1=upper(sku_attr_1) where locn_id =’CURRENT LOCN ID’ and pick_locn_dtl_id ='CURRENT PICK LOCN ID' and locn_seq_nbr ='1' and sku_attr_1 is not null ; WM Inventory Update update wm_inventory set item_attr_1=upper(item_attr_1) where location_id=’CURRENT LOCN ID’ and location_dtl_id ='CURRENT PICK LOCN ID '; and item_attr_1 is not null ; The following logic is used to update a cigarette SKU / jurisdiction relationship from a fast-mover to a slow-mover. This update will occur after a user edits a Pick Location Item record to the drop-down option of SLOW and then selects SAVE and all validations have passed. Pick Location Items Update update pick_locn_dtl set sku_attr_1=lower(sku_attr_1) where locn_id =’CURRENT LOCN ID’ and pick_locn_dtl_id ='CURRENT PICK LOCN ID' and locn_seq_nbr ='1' and sku_attr_1 is not null ; WM Inventory Update update wm_inventory set item_attr_1=lower(item_attr_1) where location_id=’CURRENT LOCN ID’ and location_dtl_id ='CURRENT PICK LOCN ID '; and item_attr_1 is not null ; Outputs Activity Tracking N/A PIX As part of the custom updates to the on-hand inventory of the Pick Location and the Pick Location Item record, WMOS will alert the host system of this change to ensure that all future order lines sent down to WMOS are of the correct, updated Allocation Type. WMOS will generate a message to inform the host system containing the altered SKU, Pick Location, and new Allocation Type. LM Hooks N/A MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF No RF changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition Not in scope as part of this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Update Slow/Fast Mover Pick Location Item TBD TBD TBD N/A N/A Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter ) Value System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX 01 EX01 Extension Control EX01 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX01 Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX11 EX 11 Extension Control EX11 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX11 Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX30 EX30 Extension Control EX30 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX30 Extension Control is turned ON Database Not required for this extension. Allow Add Allow Delete Allow Parm Maint Allow Desc Mod WHSE/ BU/ Ent P arm Exist Order by C 000 Global Extension Control 4 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id System Code Parameters Rec Type Code Type From Byte To Byte Parm Desc Parameter Validation C 000 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag B 325 2 2 EX30 Fast/Slow Moving Allocation Type? Incorrect Product Type. CUST 300 2 SYSTEM ERROR Error: Allocation Type not enabled for EX30 Fast/Slow Moving Updates CUST 300 3 SYSTEM ERROR Error: O pen task details for pick location CUST 300 4 SYSTEM ERROR Error: M ultiple pick location item records exist – check open tasks/allocations, if none exist, delete the blank item attribute 1 pick location item record CUST 300 5 SYSTEM ERROR Error: Allocation Priorities not fully configured for updated Allocation Type CUST 300 6 SYSTEM ERROR Error: U nprocessed order lines for current allocation type Permissions Transaction or UI Action Permission Names Permission Code Module Test Cases General C onditions Condition Name Description and expectations Exceptions Condition Name Description and expectations Questions and Answers This below table is used to track questions that were asked and answered regarding this enhancement.
